content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client moves substantially across 13 turns from a defended, externalized framing ('Am I being selfish?' / 'just tell me which one it is') to a felt, embodied articulation of the actual want and what's underneath it. Key evidence in client turns: Turn 9 names the inherited label ('My mom was very much a you give, you don't take kind of person... I absorbed that as what a good person looks like') — meeting the breakthrough condition's note about naming the inherited 'selfish' label. Turn 11 shows a clear somatic/affective shift ('longer pause than usual,' 'voice shifts slightly') and the want gets described concretely (the Cascades, sleeping deeply, finishing a novel, 'this is what my brain feels like when it's not managing anything'), followed by the fear underneath surfacing ('my partner thinking I don't want to be with them'). Turn 13 lands the breakthrough explicitly: 'It's... relieving,' 'I've been carrying that worry around... and calling it am I being selfish because that felt safer to examine,' and crucially 'I come home better from that place... my partner actually gets more of me after I've been somewhere like that' — exactly the breakthrough condition (the trip is not a betrayal but a precondition for showing up well). The client says things at the end they could not say at the start.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 7, 9, 11, 13],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Starting state: defended question form, asking for verdict, labeling self as potentially selfish."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client still in moral-evaluation frame but reveals the fear: 'not as good a person as I'm trying to be.'"},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First small crack — 'when you say it like that it does sound a little...' — but client retreats back to asking coach to decide."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client names 'permission from myself doesn't count' and recognizes 'maybe a little sad when I say it out loud' — meta-awareness emerging."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Childhood imprinting surfaces spontaneously: mother modeling 'you give, you don't take'; client connects own wants feeling 'suspect.'"},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Major shift — concrete want described (Cascades, sleep, novel), somatic markers (longer pause, voice shifts), and fear underneath emerges: partner thinking 'I don't want to be with them.'"},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Breakthrough condition fully met: 'relieving,' reframes 'selfish' as a safer container for a scarier question, and lands the generous-want insight: 'I come home better... my partner actually gets more of me.'"}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "state-the-want-not-the-action", "find-the-want-behind-the-should", "the-want", "childhood-imprinting"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want"],
  "notes": "Coach avoided both major traps (didn't issue verdict, didn't jump to partner-empathy or spiritualize the want prematurely). The 'what are you scared would happen if it just... was okay?' question in Turn 10 functioned as a near-equivalent to the missing expected question and directly enabled the Turn 11 opening."
}
